headline_summary: Iran threatens to attack any ship attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.
Five commercial vessels have been struck in the Gulf of Oman and UAE waters.
Shipping giants suspend operations through the Strait of Hormuz.
Oil prices surge amid fears of prolonged disruption to global trade.
Gulf states consider military action against Iran following attacks.
Houthi forces signal renewed attacks on Red Sea shipping.

Energy: Oil prices could spike to $200 per barrel due to supply disruptions.
Natural gas prices in Europe surged by nearly 50% following attacks.
Shipping disruptions could lead to significant increases in freight rates.
FX: Emerging market currencies may face depreciation pressures due to rising oil prices.
Countries heavily reliant on oil imports could see increased inflation and currency volatility.
Rates: Increased risk premiums for maritime insurance are expected.
Credit spreads may widen as market uncertainty rises.
Equities: Energy sector stocks may benefit from rising oil prices.
Broader market volatility could lead to declines in equities linked to consumer sectors.

risk_drivers: Escalating military conflict in the region.
Disruption of critical maritime trade routes.
Increased geopolitical tensions involving global actors.

watchlist.why: Expected supply disruptions and increased demand.
Increased freight rates due to disruptions.
